{{Quote|Cargo ferry ''Sela'' is carrying the TIE Defender prototypes and will rendezvous with escort carrier ''Hininbirg'' near Vinzen Neela 5.|An [[Imperial briefing officer]]|audio=6M2I1.flac|<ref name="TIE Fighter" />}}
The '''''Hininbirg''''' was a [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class escort carrier]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. Following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the ''Hininbirg'' was set to receive [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]] prototypes from the [[cargo ferry]] group [[Sela (cargo ferry group)|''Sela'']] near [[Vinzen Neela 5]] but came [[Battle of Vinzen Neela 5|under fire]] from [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] forces when it entered the area. As the [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] engaged the Rebels, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] protected the transfer operation. After receiving all of the TIE Defenders, the ''Hininbirg'' departed the area for the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

==Description==
A [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class escort carrier]], the ''Hininbirg''<ref name="TIE Fighter" /> was manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] and possessed ten [[H8 dual laser cannon]]s and a [[VL-6 warhead launch system]].<ref name="Stele Chronicles" /> The [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]] measured 500 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length, could reach a top speed of 12 [[Megalight|MGLT]], and possessed three [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight drives]] and a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. The ''Hininbirg'' had a shield rating of 180 [[SBD/Legends|SBD]] and a [[Hull/Legends|hull]] rating of 90 [[RU/Legends|RU]].<ref name="TIE Fighter" />

==History==
{{Quote|The ''Hininbirg'' will deliver the new fighters to Coruscant for the Emperor's inspection.|An Imperial briefing [[Officer/Legends|officer]], following the battle near Vinzen Neela 5|audio=BriefingOfficer-Hininbirg-TIEFighter.flac|<ref name="TIE Fighter" />}}
The ''Hininbirg'' served in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|navy]] during its [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]].<ref name="TIE Fighter" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="TIE Fighter Date">{{TIEFighterDate}}</ref> following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the ''Hininbirg'' was set to rendezvous with the [[cargo ferry]] group [[Sela (cargo ferry group)|''Sela'']] near the astronomical object [[Vinzen Neela 5]] to receive the group's [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]] prototypes. Before the Imperials could arrive in the area, however, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] forces entered the area with the aim of [[Battle of Vinzen Neela 5|ambushing]] them and disrupting the transfer operation.<ref name="TIE Fighter" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
Tasked with protecting the transfer, Imperial [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] entered the area and engaged the Rebels prior to the ''Hininbirg'' and ''Sela''{{'s}} arrivals. The carrier arrived in the midst of the battle<ref name="TIE Fighter" /> and traded fire with two [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]], ''[[Vop Hui]]'' and ''[[Mar Duun]]'', both of which attempted to destroy the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] with a [[Mine Type A|minefield]]. [[TIE pilot]] [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]] destroyed both [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]] and the [[Mine/Legends|mines]], defending the carrier from any attacking Rebel craft.<ref name="TIE Fighter CD-ROM Guide">''[[TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref> The ''Hininbirg'' ultimately survived the battle and received all prototypes from ''Sela'' intact. The ship then jumped into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and delivered the TIE Defenders to<ref name="TIE Fighter" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] would inspect them.<ref name="TIE Fighter" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Hininbirg'' first appeared in [[Totally Games]]' [[1994]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]. Featured in the sixth Tour of Duty's second mission, titled "Prevent Rebel Ambush," the ship must be protected by the player until it has received all prototypes from ''Sela'' and departed the area.<ref name="TIE Fighter" />
